articles.title: ‘Enemies Will Regret...’: Iran Vows Revenge As Trump, Netanyahu ‘Secretly Plan’ New Round Of War
articles.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf has issued a fiery warning, claiming Iran’s enemies are preparing for a new round of war despite the ongoing ceasefire. Ghalibaf said Tehran’s armed forces are fully prepared and warned that any fresh aggression would leave the enemy “regretting” its actions. He also accused the United States of trying to pressure Iran through sanctions, military threats, and naval blockades while peace talks remain stalled. The warning comes as tensions over the Strait of Hormuz and the Iran-US conflict continue to escalate across the region.

articles.title: Bhojshala verdict: Fresh plea seeks removal of 'unauthorised Islamic symbols', excavation at complex & no entry fee
articles.description: Following the Madhya Pradesh High Court's verdict recognizing Dhar's Bhojshala complex as Goddess Vagdevi's temple, Hindu petitioners have filed new pleas. They are demanding free entry for devotees, further excavation, and the opening of a locked room, asserting the court's order necessitates unrestricted access and removal of 'unauthorised Islamic symbols'.

articles.title: Maharashtra Congress reshuffle: 69 new district chiefs named ahead of 2029 polls
US House Hearing Descends Into Chaos After ‘White Supremacy’ Debate | Watch
articles.description: A heated showdown exploded inside the U.S. House during a fiery “Manufacturing Hate” hearing, as lawmakers clashed over explosive accusations tied to white supremacy. Tempers flared, voices rose, and the chamber descended into chaos as members traded sharp insults and furious rebuttals in real time. The dramatic confrontation quickly spiralled into a screaming match, exposing deep political and ideological divisions on Capitol Hill. The tense exchange has now gone viral, fuelling fresh outrage and fierce debate across the political spectrum.

articles.title: ‘I was afraid he’d stab me’: Sikh man claims self-defence in UK student murder trial
articles.description: ‘I was afraid he’d stab me’: Sikh man claims self-defence in UK student murder trial

articles.title: 'At Least 20 More Years': Pollster Pradeep Gupta's Big Call On BJP's Continuing Dominance In India
articles.description: Axis My India chief Pradeep Gupta has opined that the BJP’s political dominance in India could continue for “at least 20 years” if its governance performance remains strong.

articles.title: Row over Tamil Nadu state song being played after national anthem, Vande Mataram at ministers' oath event
articles.description: P Shanmugam said that the issue had already been raised with the CM, who clarified that Vande Mataram would be played first at all events attended by Governor

articles.title: US submits new ceasefire proposal, Iran's deliberations underway
articles.description: US submits new ceasefire proposal, Iran's deliberations underway

articles.title: BJP opens two-day Deendayal Upadhyaya training programme for State leaders
articles.description: The BJP has begun a two-day 'Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya Maha Prashikshan Abhiyan' training programme for its State, district and mandal-level leaders.

articles.title: ‘On the borderline’: Trump’s warns Tehran as Iran reviews new US proposal
articles.description: Trump had earlier assured the reporters that the negotiations between the warring nations were in their final stages and issued a warning that the United States was ready to act quickly if Tehran fails to give “100 per cent good answers”.

articles.title: BJP to contest Kerala Speaker election for first time, fields newly elected MLA Gopakumar
articles.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party is stepping into uncharted territory by nominating B B Gopakumar, an MLA from Chathannoor, for the Speaker's chair in Kerala's assembly election, which takes place this Friday. This nomination represents a pivotal moment for the BJP in the state.

articles.title: PM Modi Returns From Europe, Calls Crucial Ministerial Meeting Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has convened a crucial meeting of the Council of Ministers at Seva Teerth in Delhi, just hours after returning from his Europe tour. All Union ministers have been asked to remain in the national capital ahead of the high-level discussions, making the meeting politically significant.Sources say the ongoing West Asia crisis and its possible economic impact on India are expected to dominate the agenda. The government is closely monitoring concerns linked to crude oil prices, fuel supply chains and inflation amid escalating tensions in the region. A high-powered informal group of ministers led by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh is already reviewing the evolving situation. Home Minister Amit Shah, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri are part of the group overseeing preparedness measures. The meeting has also intensified speculation around a possible Cabinet reshuffle and expansion in the Modi 3.0 government ahead of its first anniversary on June 10. Sources indicate that the BJP leadership is reviewing ministerial performance and organisational functioning before any major exercise. The developments have made today’s meeting one of the most closely watched political and governance discussions in recent weeks

articles.title: ‘Adopt a Stray Instead’: Viral Husky Post Sparks Debate on Keeping Exotic Dogs in India’s 45°C Heat
articles.description: A viral social media post has ignited a debate in India about the ethics of owning exotic dog breeds like Siberian Huskies in hot climates. The discussion began after a user criticized a neighbor for keeping a Husky in a constantly air-conditioned room to survive 45°C heat.

articles.title: Kerala Assembly: BJP to contest Speaker election, fields B.B. Gopakumar
articles.description: BJP fields B.B. Gopakumar as its candidate for Kerala Assembly Speaker election, marking a historic first for the party.

articles.title: Congress returns to Tamil Nadu government after 59 years as Vijay expands Cabinet
articles.description: 23 MLAs, including 21 from TVK and 2 from Congress, took oath in TN CM Vijay’s cabinet expansion. This marks Congress's return to the state government after nearly six decades.

articles.title: PM Modi gifts Assam’s famed Muga silk stole to Italy PM Giorgia Meloni
articles.description: PM Modi and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni held bilateral talks in Rome and reiterated their commitment to strengthening the India-Italy strategic partnership.

articles.title: Sonia, Rahul, Priyanka Gandhi pay tribute to Rajiv Gandhi on 35th death anniversary at Vir Bhumi
articles.description: Sonia Gandhi, Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge paid tribute to former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi on his 35th death anniversary at Vir Bhumi in New Delhi.

articles.title: Falta repoll: BJP's Panda confident of victory amid festive voting
articles.description: BJP's Falta candidate Debangshu Panda called the repolling atmosphere 'festive' and expressed confidence in a massive victory. He mocked his TMC rival, Jahangir Khan, and claimed the BJP would win by a margin of over one and a half lakh votes.

articles.title: Rajiv Gandhi death anniversary: PM Modi, Congress leaders pay tribute
articles.description: PM Narendra Modi and senior Congress leaders including Sonia Gandhi, Rahul Gandhi, and Mallikarjun Kharge paid tribute to former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi on his 35th death anniversary. Tributes were paid at his memorial, Vir Bhumi, in New Delhi.

articles.title: CM Vijay expands cabinet; Rajesh Kumar, P Vishwanathan take oath
articles.description: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay expanded his cabinet as Rajesh Kumar and P Vishwanathan took oath, raising the ministry's strength to 33. The council includes 21 MLAs from TVK and 2 from Congress. Congress leaders lauded the formation of a "secular government".

articles.title: India slams Pakistan, calls out 'long-tainted genocidal record' at UNSC
articles.description: India called out Pakistan's "long-tainted" record of genocidal acts, telling the UN Security Council that the country's inhuman conduct reflects its attempts over decades to externalise internal failures through acts of violence within and beyond its border. Speaking at the Annual UNSC Open Debate on 'Protection of civilians in armed conflict' on Wednesday, India's Permanent Representative to the UN Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni said, "It is ironic that Pakistan, with its long-tainted record of genocidal acts, has chosen to refer to issues that are strictly internal to India." Parvathaneni's remarks came after Pakistan's representative raised the issue of Jammu and Kashmir at the debate. Raising the issue of Pakistan's strikes on Afghanistan earlier this year, he said, "The world has not forgotten that it was during the holy month of Ramadan in March this year, at a time of peace, reflection, and mercy, that Pakistan conducted a barbaric airstrike on the Omid Addiction Treatment ..

articles.title: Agarkar's justification on Shami's snub called 'bakwaas', ex-IND batter asks 'would you treat Bumrah the same way?'
articles.description: Both team composition and injuries played a part in keeping Mohammed Shami out of reckoning for India’s series against Afghanistan that is scheduled for June.

articles.title: UP man strangles daughter over affair, carries semi-conscious body on bike to bury her
articles.description: Family members were allegedly against daughter's relationship with a young man from the same village, but Shivani had continued speaking to the youth over the phone for nearly a year.

articles.title: Punjab witnessing ‘pro-incumbency’ wave in favour of AAP, says Kejriwal
articles.description: Speaking to the media after he reached Ludhiana on a two-day visit to the state to hold meetings with the party leaders in preparation for the state assembly elections

articles.title: PM Modi To Chair High-Stakes Council Of Ministers’ Meet At Bharat Mandapam Today
articles.description: The Prime Minister will chair a meeting of the Council of Ministers at Bharat Mandapam during which he will review the government's performance, schemes, infrastructure projects.

articles.title: Falta repolling: Voting begins in West Bengal's high-stakes seat amid tight security
articles.description: Falta, which falls within Abhishek Banerjee’s bastion of Diamond Harbour, reflected the intense on-ground political heat during the West Bengal Assembly polls. Apart from authorities finding attempts to tamper with footage captured by web cameras installed at several polling stations, allegations of EVM manipulation were reported at a Falta booth, eventually prompting the Election Commission to order repolling.

articles.title: 3 weeks on, no breakthrough in first case registered under new anti-sacrilege Act
articles.description: The anti-sacrilege bill, which proposed amendments to the original 2008 Act, was passed unanimously by the Punjab Vidhan Sabha during a special one-day session on April 13

articles.title: Dharmendra Pradhan Monitors Social Media Crackdown, Orders Strict Vigilance Ahead Of NEET Re-Exam
articles.description: Dharmendra Pradhan directed authorities and social media platforms to crack down on fake NEET paper leak claims and online misinformation ahead of the June 21 re-examination.

articles.title: CJP overtakes BJP: ‘Cockroach Janta Party’ surpasses 9 million Instagram followers in just four days
articles.description: Discover how Abhijeet Dipke's satirical Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) crossed 9M Instagram followers, outgrowing major Indian political accounts in just 4 days.

articles.title: MLA Ayali quits SAD rebel faction, set to join Amritpal’s outfit
articles.description: Senior Akali leader and Dakha MLA Manpreet Singh Ayali on Wednesday resigned from the Shiromani Akali Dal breakaway faction SAD (Punar Surjit) and hinted at joining the Akali Dal Waris Punjab De, a fledgling political outfit headed by jailed Khadoor Sahib MP Amritpal Singh

articles.title: Tamil Nadu's Vijay-led TVK govt expands cabinet, 23 MLAs to take oath as ministers today
articles.description: Twenty-three newly elected MLAs will join the Tamil Nadu State Cabinet. Governor has approved their appointments. The swearing-in ceremony is scheduled for Thursday morning in Chennai. This expansion is the second major cabinet exercise by the Vijay-led TVK government. Several MLAs from various constituencies are set to take oath.

articles.title: ‘Justice Will Be Served’: White House Shares Post Claiming Trump ‘Neutralised’ America’s Enemies
articles.description: White House X post touts Trump neutralizing enemies, labels Maduro arrested, Khamenei and Abu Bilal al-Muniki killed, Raul Castro indicted, sparking online backlash over tone.

articles.title: Caste and crisis of fraternity
articles.description: The persistence of caste supremacy, to the extent of undermining the credibility and legitimacy of institutions, such as the Supreme Court and the office of the Chief Justice of India (CJI), is a serious concern. Such incidents are symptoms of deeply rooted caste-based discrimination and marginalisation.

articles.title: Dalit leaders divided over recording caste in census
articles.description: Grandsons of Dalit leader Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar are appealing to the community to mention their original caste in the caste column of the census form, while identifying themselves as ‘Buddhists’ in the column relating to religion. According to them, this would safeguard the social, political and financial benefits available to Scheduled Castes

articles.title: UP may keep village pradhans ‘in power’ even after their tenure
articles.description: Government is actively considering appointing village pradhans as administrators following the expiry of five-year term of existing gram panchayats on May 26

articles.title: Presidential Records Act ruling: Can Donald Trump ignore the Presidential Records Act? Judge says the White House must follow the law
articles.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to comply with the Presidential Records Act, rejecting a Justice Department opinion that deemed the law unconstitutional. U.S. District Judge John Bates ruled the act is likely constitutional and found a substantial risk of non-compliance.

articles.title: Aimee Bock led one of the largest frauds in history; she's blaming Minnesota for not stopping her

articles.title: India not a 'Dharamshala'; detect, delete, deport infiltrators: BJP
articles.description: Union Minister Sukanta Majumdar reiterated the Centre's "detect, delete and deport" policy on illegal infiltration, stating India is not a 'Dharamshala'. He said CAA is for refugees, but others will have to go back to their countries.
